properties.evidence: install.sh is checksum-verified and pulls published binaries (no rebuild), and reflow2_init.py updates a project in place — but the installer is not yet reachable from a stable public one-liner URL, there is no single self-update command, and the release cadence is not yet frequent-and-minor. The delta is the backlog's frictionless-update item.

properties.description: Read requirements, functions and structure back out of a running system.
properties.name: Recover a design from an existing system

properties.description: Score function-to-structure allocation; cluster coupling.
properties.name: Analyse and propose allocation

properties.description: Sum contributions along the thread; refuse a total it cannot honestly compute.
properties.name: Roll up a budget against a constraint

properties.description: Snapshot prior state at an epoch, then edit — axis Z.
properties.name: Record a change

properties.description: Diff two export documents (or the live graph against one) into added / removed / changed relative to a named base, banded into design content vs bookkeeping, property-level on changes.
properties.name: Compare two as-designed records

properties.description: Find what the design has not decided yet, ranked.
properties.name: Detect gaps

properties.description: Assess nodes on dimensions and detect decline.
properties.name: Track quality over time

properties.description: Distinguish a design that matches reality from one nobody has checked.
properties.name: Say when a claim was last confirmed

properties.description: Seed a project, its requirements and capabilities, refusing to clobber an existing graph.
properties.name: Bootstrap a design from a brief

properties.description: Propose content-free structural fixes, then apply atomically.
properties.name: Repair structure

properties.description: Find missing intermediates and level mismatches — axis Y.
properties.name: Check decomposition levels

properties.description: Multi-pass LLM extraction with provenance.
properties.name: Extract a design from freeform text

properties.description: One command sets up or refreshes the design environment.
properties.name: Install into a consumer project

properties.description: One cheap call returning the coherence loop's outstanding debt as an ordered to-do list, computed from graph state alone.
properties.name: Say what the loop is owed

properties.description: Every capability as a typed tool for an agent.
properties.name: Serve the loop over MCP

properties.description: Represent an ordered flow of activities with roles on its edges.
properties.name: Model a process, not only a product

properties.description: Move a design across machines and versions.
properties.name: Export and import a design

properties.description: Walk the golden thread to compute a blast radius.
properties.name: Propagate impact

properties.description: Questions outlive the session; answers stay visible.
properties.name: Remember what was asked

properties.description: Compare registered artifacts against observed files and report divergence.
properties.name: Reconcile against what was built

properties.description: Compare the design against what is actually deployed.
properties.name: Reconcile against what is running

properties.description: Compare recorded verification status against a real test run.
properties.name: Reconcile against what was proven

properties.description: One rollup answering 'what should I look at?'.
properties.name: Say where the design stands

properties.description: BM25 keyword search over every fulltext property, ranked, type-scopable.
properties.name: Find design nodes by what they say

properties.description: Stamp and check, refusing a graph from the future.
properties.name: Say which reflow2 wrote a graph

properties.description: A typed vocabulary of node and edge types, validated on write, over a pluggable graph storage backend — the foundation everything else hangs off.
properties.name: Schema-validated graph store

properties.description: Turn a gap into a question in the user's words.
properties.name: Ask the user a plain question

properties.description: Tell a client what types exist and what may join them.

Export content hash + lineage chain + compare ancestry (AT-proto capture)

BL-26 (recorded late): the DOCUMENTS write side — artifact.rs gained the documents() constructor so a descriptive file can say what it explains. The design moved with the code in commit 2d86ccb (2026-07-20); this event records the design side that session omitted, found by the 2026-07-21 reconcile sweep.

BL-48 + BL-49: result contract hardened at the tool boundary — graph_report_markdown returns prose as text (never a bare string in structuredContent; ok_json wraps any scalar), propagate answers with a bounded summary by default (full dump behind full=true), export_graph writes to a file on request

BL-50: three tool-boundary paper cuts closed — integer literals widen losslessly to schema floats at the core write seam (confidence: 1 accepted, range check kept), add_change_event takes an affected list and draws its CHANGED edges atomically (delete results named {deleted}), describe_schema counts half-exact edges so CHANGED reads as the modelled fit; kit documents a SessionStart hook making the where-am-i ritual mechanical

BL-57: the tool boundary tells the truth — dyno_err maps caller mistakes to invalid_params not internal_error (one choke point, ~60 tools); every request struct denies unknown fields (a typo'd param is rejected, not swallowed — schemars publishes additionalProperties:false); export_graph refuses to overwrite without opt-in and reports the resolved path; the serve path gets the single-writer lock explanation; get_node returns one named {node} shape both ways; withdraw_gap_acknowledgement aligns on {withdrawn}

BL-58: core silent-failure batch — ingest re-ingest merges instead of resetting (upsert), snapshots serialize sorted for byte-stable exports, propagate_change errors on a missing event instead of returning empty, apply_heal is one atomic batch across all operations, swallowed edge-creation errors now surface, budget rejects non-finite contributions and reports a provable overrun instead of Incomplete, integer widening rejects the i64::MAX saturation edge, truncated_beyond_depth documented as the frontier lower-bound, drift skips the dangling edge for undocumented additions, missing-intermediate gaps get distinct ids per edge type, a reused ingest fragment_id is refused, node_type_index scans in sorted order

BL-63: snapshots capture design edges — an edge move keeps its history. Snapshot.edges (optional, sorted, bookkeeping excluded) added beside state; parse_snapshot_edges/SnapshotEdge in core; CRUD skills updated to record-first-then-delete.

BL-66: the consumer CI coherence gate — reflow2_check.py reads the committed export, rehashes registered artifacts, runs detect_gaps, and exits non-zero on unaccepted drift or an open anchored gap at/above threshold; ci-gate skill + CI snippet ship in the kit; the tarball carries the script.
BL-69: SPOF measured on the as-built operational network
BL-71 rung c: design-vs-design diff (compare_designs core op + MCP tool + CLI)
BL-73: component-granularity verification — the computed third state
BL-74 rungs c+b: loop_status debt rollup + loop hints in write-tool results
BL-74 rung a: the loop-nudge hook — the trigger fired by the harness's own events
BL-75: requirement certainty derived and rendered (user-confirmed / asserted / recovered)

Deep-review tier-1 fixes (BL-53..56): HEAL refuses self-loop DUPLICATES merges (a sanctioned self-merge deleted the node with no undo); the installer proves file ownership by hash manifest — user edits are kept and reported, obsolete kit files pruned only when untouched, malformed configs left alone without crashing; install.sh fails loud instead of silently or falsely succeeding; release publishes only after every asset is verified attached; the smoke harness refuses to wipe an existing graph directory without --wipe

BL-47 + BL-46: merge survivor ranks unset provenance below explicit authored; a colliding edge keeps the survivor's properties; create_node on an existing id merges instead of replacing (the revise-design contract, now real)

Reconcile genesis-stub survivor nodes with the committed model (user-approved 2026-07-20): merge cap:install→cap:kit, cap:artifacts→cap:reconcile-built, req:past-preserved→req:intent-preserved; wire cap:store; satisfy req:platform via cap:kit; verify cap:adopt from the storyflow trial

v0.5.0 as-released model: rel:v050 recorded (33 artifacts frozen at tag v0.5.0 + the skills tree), deployed active on the developer machine; rel:v040 retired and its deployment declaration withdrawn (upgraded in place); INCLUDES joined the traceability table so a release is part of the design network rather than an island
consumer kit
properties.purpose: What gets installed into a project being designed.

properties.name: reflow2-mcp
properties.purpose: The agent-facing MCP surface over one graph.

properties.decision: detect_gaps sorts anchored gaps before project-level phase nudges, severity within each band.
properties.name: A gap that names nodes outranks a phase nudge
properties.rationale: A named gap says something is wrong NOW; a nudge says what comes next. Ranking 'next' above 'broken' made three brownfield trials do the useless thing first. Nudges are demoted, never suppressed. Decided 2026-07-19 (https://claude.ai/code/session_013aumVdHrRHu24cLirn6Cam); evidence: gap-surfacing disciplines 3 and 8.

properties.decision: possible_duplicate is a DETECT gap; HEAL merges only on a human-drawn DUPLICATES edge.
properties.name: Suspected duplicates are asked, never merged
properties.rationale: apply_heal deletes a node with no undo, so merge is safe only because the endpoints were asserted; a heuristic must not drive it. A gap can be acknowledged, a HEAL defect cannot be dismissed. Decided 2026-07-19 (https://claude.ai/code/session_013aumVdHrRHu24cLirn6Cam); evidence: 3dtictactoe trial, BL-27/BL-29.

properties.decision: Requirement certainty is a computed reading, never a stored third axis: status accepted/met → user-confirmed; status deferred/dropped → settled out (also the user's word); status proposed → asserted (provenance authored/planned/imported) or recovered (provenance inferred/reconciled/healed). The load-bearing doctrine: an agent captures requirements at proposed and ONLY the user's answer moves the status — accepted, met, deferred and dropped are all user-only verbs. Reports render the certainty breakdown directly so no session reconstructs it in prose.
properties.name: A Requirement's certainty is derived from status × provenance; every move off proposed is the user's word
properties.rationale: User decided all three axes 2026-07-22 (BL-75 vocabulary session, from their fleet trial: where-am-i had to caveat user-confirmed vs recovered in prose every session). Derived over stored for the BL-73 reason — a third stored property can contradict the two axes that already span the space, and the backlog's own warning was 'decide deliberately, not additively'. Every-move-off-proposed rather than accepted-only because an agent dropping a requirement on its own judgment is exactly the silent decision the loop exists to prevent — and this is BL-12 sketch idea 2's example made real ('a requirement moves to accepted only on the human's say-so'), enforced culturally in the skills and the tool description today; the mechanical half stays with BL-41. 'Recovered' over the trial's 'adopted' because it matches the graph's own vocabulary (cap:adopt: 'Recover a design from an existing system') and names the fact, not the workflow. No schema change: the whole defect was the read side — the second time this week (BL-73) the vocabulary was already sufficient and unread.

properties.decision: A capability's verification state is three-valued: verified (a passing check of its own), component_verified (no check of its own, but a component it is allocated to carries a passing one — computed at read time, never written), and unchecked. The coverage line reports the third state as its own clause; detect_gaps replaces the N per-capability unverified alarms with ONE component_granularity_verification gap per carrying component at 0.35 — listing the riding capabilities, acknowledgeable once; status_contradiction accepts component-granularity proof for a verified status claim; loop_status counts component_verified as proven. A failing component suite carries nothing: passing-is-verified holds at every granularity.
properties.name: Component-granularity verification is a computed third state, one question per component
properties.rationale: User decided all three axes 2026-07-22 (BL-73 vocabulary session, from their own fleet's field trial): a brownfield adopt with real per-service suites plus a 137-file integration suite read as '0/20 capabilities verified' and cost 21 near-identical acknowledge writes. Computed state over a bulk-attest write because the graph should record exactly what was checked — the component — and derive what that means one hop away; manufactured capability-level VERIFIES edges would be indistinguishable from direct checks forever after. The word component_verified because it says where the check lives. One gap per component because the residual question ('is component granularity enough for these?') is genuinely per-component and deserves to be asked once, not N times — the BL-42 lesson again: a loud detector needs a different question, not a tuned number. The write side needed nothing: VERIFIES accepts any target and even carries an unused coverage enum — the whole defect was the read side.

properties.decision: The design's authorship identity is a new first-class Contributor node type (kind: person / automated_agent / organization), kept separate from the existing Actor. Actor is who the designed system SERVES (boundary-facing domain content); Contributor is who authors and decides the design ITSELF. The first rung is the authorship seed: an AUTHORED_BY edge (any design node → Contributor, role author/reviewer/approver) — the structured "who" behind provenance's "how", replacing the free-text Requirement.source. AUTHORED_BY is deliberately NOT a traceability edge (absent from nodes.rs structural_rule), so authorship is metadata and never enlarges a blast radius. Later rungs, each needing the user on vocabulary: CLAIMS (BL-44 node/cluster claims, advisory-first), ACTS_FOR (agent-acts-for-person, git's author/committer split), alternative-authorship (BL-70), and signing the export content_hash with a Contributor key (BL-41 mechanical half). No "unauthored node" detector yet — it would N-alarm on every existing node.
properties.name: Design-authorship is a Contributor, distinct from an Actor; the seed is AUTHORED_BY
properties.rationale: User chose the identity keystone as the next most important work (2026-07-22), and decided both axes: a new type over overloading Actor, and authorship-first over jumping to claims. First-class node over a bare property because a claim expires and authorship belongs on axis-Z — a property is invisible to history. One identity mechanism because the backlog's own warning is that building it for claims alone would be "the recurring lesson in reverse": the same who-is-this is needed by BL-44 (claims), BL-70 (alternative authorship), BL-41 (the mechanical half of requirement-certainty, cultural today), and BL-12 sketch 2 (who may assert what). Contributor separate from Actor because they have different lifecycles — a UAF model keeps who-serves and who-makes apart. The git exploration (2026-07-22) independently ratified the shape: git's author-vs-committer split is exactly the deferred ACTS_FOR rung, and signing the record hash is BL-41's mechanical half. Additive schema change (28 node / 55 edge types); a new-binary graph is refused loudly by a pre-Contributor binary via the count-based provenance check (BL-19), so no schema_version bump.

properties.decision: A design-vs-design diff (compare_designs) exists as a core op exposed as both an MCP tool and a CLI flag. It compares two export documents, or the live graph against one document. Findings are directional — added / removed / changed relative to an explicitly named base — with property-level detail on changed nodes, grouped into two bands: design content vs the supporting/bookkeeping layer (ChangeEvents, DriftEvents, Fragments, Questions). It reports divergence between two as-designed records and never judges which side is right; the word 'drift' stays reserved for design-vs-reality.
properties.name: Design-vs-design comparison is directional, banded, and lives in the core
properties.rationale: User decided all four axes 2026-07-21 (BL-71 rung c vocabulary session). Directional because all three consumers have a real base — the committed record (the BL-71 clobber tripwire), the main branch (BL-70 branch-by-file comparison), the state a claim was made against (BL-12 merge) — while AoA can still read the report neutrally or run it both ways. Banded because the real 2026-07-21 divergence was 3 Decisions and 8 Requirements buried under ~20 bookkeeping nodes; a flat list hides exactly what matters. Core op (not a kit script) because deterministic design computation belongs in the tested core; CLI flag so CI and no-server contexts reach it, like --import/--export. It is the read side of the rung a+b upsert-layering, the sibling of the reconcile family — which compares design against reality, hence the separate word: divergence, not drift.

properties.decision: cmp:export and ifc:graph-export are the only route from a design to the kit converter (cmp:init) and to any other machine or reflow2 version, and they stay that way. Both single_point_of_failure warnings are accepted as intentional, not resolved by adding a second export path.
properties.name: The export document is an accepted single door, so its chain is an accepted SPOF
properties.rationale: dec:repo-file-embedded made the deterministic export the durable, diffable record, and req:survives-upgrade makes export-with-the-old-build / import-with-the-new THE migration mechanism — a single door is the feature, because a second export path would be a second source of truth for what the design is, and byte-identical determinism (req:persistence: a backup under git shows what actually changed) is only a meaningful promise about one format. The detector is right that severing this chain cuts cmp:init off from the design; that fragility is the price of one canonical portability format, knowingly paid. Accepted 2026-07-21 while dispositioning BL-69's corrected findings; sibling of dec:service-spof-accepted and dec:graph-spof-accepted.

properties.decision: Every export carries a content_hash — sha256 over the canonical sorted JSON of the design content only (graph_id, nodes, edges), excluding the stamp and chain fields, so the same design hashes identically whichever build wrote it. A prev_content_hash records file lineage: when an export overwrites an existing export file, the chain advances to the old file's content hash — but only when content actually changed, so two exports of an unchanged design stay byte-identical. The full hash is carried (not truncated like display checksums) because this is tamper evidence. compare_designs reports ancestry from the chain (other succeeds base / base succeeds other / siblings of a common parent / unknown); import reports an integrity mismatch loudly; the CI gate fails on a committed export that does not match its own hash. Documents predating the hash stay importable and comparable — absence is reported, never an error.
properties.name: The export proves itself: content hash + file-lineage chain, from AT proto's self-authenticating repos
properties.rationale: User directed 2026-07-21 after the AT Protocol comparison: atproto repos are self-authenticating (signed commits over a content-addressed root), and reflow2's export was already deterministic — the hash was well-defined and simply never computed. Content-only hashing (stamp excluded) was proven necessary the same day: the committed export and the live export had identical content but different writers (0.8.0 script vs 0.7.0-era session server), and they must fingerprint the same. The chain lives at the file-write seam because the committed export file IS the durable record whose lineage matters; the graph itself must not be mutated by a read-only export. Ancestry is what turns compare_designs from 'what diverged' into 'was the divergence made in ignorance of the base or in defiance of it' — BL-12 sketch idea 1's question, computable for the one-step case. Signing (identity) is deliberately deferred until a second writer is real; a hash chain without keys is already tamper-evident against accident and hand-editing, which is today's actual threat model.

properties.decision: cmp:graph is the single store handle everything else hangs off, and it stays that way. The single_point_of_failure warning on it is accepted as intentional, not resolved by adding redundancy.
properties.name: The graph handle is an accepted single point of failure
properties.rationale: The architecture is explicit (AGENTS.md): DesignGraph is 'the single handle everything else hangs off' — one schema-validated CRUD surface so every coherence-loop module speaks to one store one way. Removing it strands schema, search and vocabulary (each with its file) plus the export chain, which is the BL-69 detector telling the truth about the spine. Redundancy is incoherent here twice over: these are modules compiled into one binary (they cannot fail independently of cmp:graph at run time — the same reasoning as the F6 library filter, which cannot see cmp:graph only because it provides no Interface node), and a second graph handle would mean two write paths to one store, exactly the incoherence the single handle exists to prevent. Accepted 2026-07-21 while dispositioning BL-69's corrected findings; sibling of dec:service-spof-accepted.

properties.decision: loop_status reports what the coherence loop is owed — anchored gaps never surfaced, questions waiting or answered-but-unwritten, structural defects, capabilities claiming realized/verified with no passing check, drift awaiting disposition, claims never examined — computed entirely from current graph state. reflow2 does not track when tools were run: phase nudges are excluded (guidance, not debt), and the write tools carry static loop_hint pointers at the next loop step in their own results.
properties.name: Loop debt is computed from graph state, never from run history
properties.rationale: From the StoryFlow fleet trial (BL-74): under operational load the agent kept bookkeeping via raw tools while the loop skills silently stopped — 'a design-discipline tool that depends on being remembered will lose to operational urgency every time; fire on a trigger, not on virtue.' Run-history ('no detect_gaps since Tuesday') is not honestly computable here: the core takes no clock, and looking-is-not-writing is doctrine — recording reads so observation could be tracked would make looking mutate the record. Outstanding debt is the state-based equivalent and the actionable truth regardless of who ran what when. The hint rides the tool result the agent already reads (zero extra round-trip to see it), and loop_status is the cheap computation a client hook can fire on its own events — rung a of the BL-74 ladder builds on exactly this call. Decided 2026-07-21.

properties.decision: When HEAL merges DUPLICATES-asserted nodes, the survivor is picked by provenance rank (authored > planned > imported > reconciled > inferred > healed); node id breaks ties. Pre-provenance graphs are unchanged.
properties.name: Merge survivor is chosen by provenance, id breaks ties
properties.rationale: User decided option 2 for BL-29 (2026-07-20): provenance is the closest thing the graph has to 'who asserted this and how deliberately'. Refinement from the 2026-07-20 self-adopt live session: a node with UNSET provenance defaults to authored, which made two genesis stubs tie against authored+verified survivors and nearly win on alphabetical id — set provenance explicitly on both sides before proposing a merge, and consider ranking unset below authored in a future revision.

properties.decision: single_point_of_failure candidates are Components, Interfaces, Resources, Environments.
properties.name: Only things that operate can be single points of failure
properties.rationale: The suggested fix is add_redundancy, and redundancy is only coherent for running parts; a golden thread converges on intent by design, so intent hubs are the thread working. 22 of 22 false positives cleared, 4 true survivors. Decided 2026-07-19 (https://claude.ai/code/session_013aumVdHrRHu24cLirn6Cam); BL-5.

properties.decision: verification_coverage counts passing checks; a failing check is its own 0.8 gap.
properties.name: Verified means a check that passes, not one that exists
properties.rationale: A failing test used to satisfy the gap that asked for a test, and passing vs failing were byte-identical to every diagnostic — counting test nodes while ignoring test results. Decided 2026-07-19 (https://claude.ai/code/session_013aumVdHrRHu24cLirn6Cam); evidence: phase-coverage trial, BL-30.

properties.decision: RocksDB directory beside the repo, exports as the durable, diffable record.
properties.name: The graph lives as a repo file, embedded — not a service
properties.rationale: The service's strongest argument (concurrency) is hypothetical while there is one writer; it would put the user's design on a machine they do not control and is permanent operational cost. Reopening conditions are written down. Decided 2026-07-18 (surface-plan.md); BL-12/BL-15 carry the consequences.

properties.decision: Per capability: drifting / confirmed / unexamined, with the disposition counts visible.
properties.name: The confirmation ledger reports claim history, never judges a claim
properties.rationale: Five design_holds claims with zero design edits is the erosion signature and the ledger makes it legible — but judging a specific claim false is semantic, and a deterministic detector would fire on every stable design with cosmetic churn (the unexpected_coupling lesson). Decided 2026-07-19 (https://claude.ai/code/session_013aumVdHrRHu24cLirn6Cam); BL-35.

properties.decision: cmp:service is the sole bridge between the reflow2-mcp subsystem and both the core and the kit, and it stays that way. The single_point_of_failure warning on it is accepted as intentional, not resolved by adding redundancy.
properties.name: The single MCP service is an accepted single point of failure
properties.rationale: req:invocation mandates one reflow2-mcp binary spoken to over stdio as the entire user-facing surface; a redundant second service would contradict that 'one door' design and buys nothing while there is a single local writer (dec:repo-file-embedded). Articulation-point analysis of the component/interface dependency graph (2026-07-21 self-host review) confirms cmp:service is a genuine cut vertex — removing it disconnects the MCP surface — so the finding is true, and the right disposition is to accept it rather than fix it. Contrast cmp:flow, flagged by the same detector but NOT a cut vertex (BL-69). Redundancy is the wrong fix; this SPOF is the architecture working as intended.

properties.decision: Every capability is designed as a check one party places on another; none does another's job.
properties.name: The LLM speaks, the graph remembers and counts, the human decides
properties.rationale: The parties are not interchangeable: the LLM cannot be trusted with arithmetic, memory or its own confidence; the graph cannot judge meaning; the human cannot remember everything or notice slow drift. The deterministic core computes, the graph carries claims and their audit trail, questions route judgment to the human. docs/partnership.md maps each known LLM failure mode to its mechanism. Decided 2026-07-19 (https://claude.ai/code/session_013aumVdHrRHu24cLirn6Cam).

properties.decision: set_artifact_checksum requires a disposition: design_holds (a dated claim) or design_updated (naming the design-side ChangeEvent, linked to the artifact).
properties.name: Silent drift-accept does not exist
properties.rationale: 'Accept the file, leave the design alone, say nothing' is how a design erodes into fiction over N legitimate fix cycles while reporting zero gaps — the failure that sank the original reflow. Decided 2026-07-19 (https://claude.ai/code/session_013aumVdHrRHu24cLirn6Cam); evidence: erosion trials, BL-33.

properties.decision: Renderers may only emit what the graph states; everything else is confessed as a finding.
properties.name: A view is a projection of the graph; renderer fill-ins are defects
properties.rationale: UAF/DoDAF doctrine from the author: the graph stores all design detail, the agent only renders viewpoints. If rendering requires extrapolation, something is missing inside reflow2 — almost always. Decided 2026-07-19 (https://claude.ai/code/session_013aumVdHrRHu24cLirn6Cam); render_views.py, BL-40.

properties.name: Post-v0.5.0 hardening: self-adopt reconciliation, BL-46..50, the as-released model (2026-07-20)

properties.name: Reflow 2.0
properties.objective: Keep a design coherent from concept through operations

properties.answer: Covered — link it. The v0.5.0 published binaries (3 platforms via release.yml) plus install.sh are exactly this promise; draw SATISFIES from cap:kit and fold the no-rebuild intent into its description.
properties.context_setter: Nothing satisfies requirement “Runs on Linux and macOS developer machines”
properties.question: You promised reflow2 works on both Linux and Mac laptops without users sitting through a long rebuild for every update — but nothing in the design currently claims to deliver that promise. Is it actually covered by the published binaries and install work you just finished, still on the to-do list, or no longer a promise you're making?

properties.answer: Keep it and wire it up: promote to authored, allocate to the schema/graph/storage components, add a Verification backed by the real test suite. It is the foundation, not a stub artifact.
properties.question: How will you prove the design store only ever keeps valid, well-formed records — or is this the same store your design already describes and tests elsewhere under a different name?

properties.answer: Same thing as the authored 'Reconcile against what was built' — merge them (user: "go with your recommendation on all the duplicates"). Keep the stub's write-side wording (checksummed registration) in the survivor.
properties.question: How will you check that comparing the recorded files against what's really on disk actually works — or is this the same ability your design already covers and tests under the name 'reconcile against what was built'?

properties.answer: Record the storyflow brownfield adopt trial (2643 files, 5 true findings) as the proof, with its real outcome; this session's self-adopt run is a second data point.
properties.context_setter: Nothing verifies “Recover a design from an existing system”
properties.question: You've now tried recovering a design from an existing codebase on several real projects — what would count as proof that this works well enough to rely on, and should one of those trial runs be recorded as that proof?

properties.answer: Same thing as the authored 'Install into a consumer project' — merge them (user: "go with your recommendation on all the duplicates"). Keep the four-harness / merge-not-overwrite detail in the survivor.
properties.question: How will you confirm the one-command setup really installs everything into a user's project without breaking their existing configuration — or is this the same ability your design already covers and tests under the name 'install into a consumer project'?

properties.description: Superseded by v0.5.0 on the developer machine (deployment declaration withdrawn as rolled_back, 2026-07-20 — the binary was upgraded in place, not reverted).
properties.name: v0.4.0

properties.description: First release cut from the public repo; first live run of release.yml (3-platform binaries + kit tarball, checksum-verified install.sh). Surface change from v0.4.0: documents tool; graph model unchanged. Never published: its release run sat stuck in the macos-x86_64 queue for 11h; superseded by v0.6.0 cut from current main (2026-07-21).
properties.name: v0.5.0

properties.description: The first release to actually reach a user (v0.5.0's run never published). Carried the deep-review tier-1 fixes and BL-57 tool-boundary honesty. Superseded in place by v0.6.1.
properties.name: v0.6.0

properties.description: Patch: the BL-58 core silent-failure batch (12 doctrine/correctness fixes). Superseded in place by v0.7.0.
properties.name: v0.6.1

properties.description: Minor: Snapshot.edges (BL-63, snapshots capture design edges), single_point_of_failure measured on the as-built operational network (BL-69), and the consumer CI coherence gate reflow2_check.py + ci-gate skill (BL-66) — the kit tarball's first second tool. Superseded in place by v0.8.0.
properties.name: v0.7.0

properties.description: Minor: compare_designs, the design-vs-design diff (BL-71 rung c) — the reconcile family's sibling for two as-designed records, as a core op + MCP tool + --diff CLI. No schema change. First release whose manifest carries compare.rs. Superseded in place by v0.9.0.
properties.name: v0.8.0

properties.description: Minor: loop_status + the write-tool loop_hints + the loop-nudge hook (BL-74, adoption-critical, from the first extensive field trial), and the export hash-chain (dec:export-hash-chain, from the AT Protocol comparison — the committed design record is now tamper-evident in CI). No schema change. First release whose kit tarball carries loop_nudge.py.
properties.name: v0.9.0
A system that already exists can be brought under design control
properties.statement: Requirements, functions and structure can be recovered from a running system.

properties.name: An agent can drive the whole loop
properties.statement: Every capability is reachable from a coding agent over one surface.

properties.name: As-designed vs as-built divergence is detectable
properties.statement: Real files on disk are registered as checksummed Artifacts realizing capabilities, so drift between the design and the build is visible rather than assumed.

properties.name: Design stays coherent across its lifecycle
properties.statement: When anything changes in any phase, the ripples are found and surfaced.

properties.name: Design anything, not only software
properties.statement: Software, hardware, a document, a process — the vocabulary stays neutral.

properties.name: The core is deterministic and LLM-free
properties.statement: The runtime core must be free of LLM and provider dependencies and neutral to the interaction surface; LLM reasoning enters only behind a sync, object-safe seam.

properties.name: The driving agents are coding agents, not humans
properties.statement: The primary consumers are Claude Code and grok build (plus OpenCode/Copilot), which reach the system as MCP tools plus an installed skill kit. The human is reached only through the agent.

properties.name: Install is one command, update is one word
properties.statement: A consumer installs reflow2 with a single command from a stable public URL (curl | bash or the platform equivalent) and updates with a single short command; releases are frequent and very minor, so updating is routine and cheap rather than an event. Modeled on the Claude Code installer/update experience the user named as the target (2026-07-20).

properties.name: Every artifact traces to the intent it serves
properties.statement: Traceability runs from concept through operations without a break.

properties.name: Gaps become questions, not guesses
properties.statement: Where the design is underspecified, the system must ask the human in plain language and record the answer, rather than silently choosing a default.

properties.name: The past is never overwritten
properties.statement: Updating the design to match the build must not erase what was intended.

properties.name: Invoked as an MCP server over stdio
properties.statement: The user-facing surface is the reflow2-mcp binary spoken to over stdio, installed into a consumer project by tools/reflow2_init.py, which merges rather than overwrites existing agent configuration.

properties.name: The user never needs systems engineering
properties.statement: The graph carries the discipline; the user answers plain questions.

properties.name: No silent fallbacks or drops
properties.statement: Failures and skipped work are surfaced loudly, never swallowed.

properties.name: The design persists in-repo alongside the code
properties.statement: The graph lives at ./.reflow2/graph inside the consumer project, so the design travels with the repo; export/import is deterministic so a backup under git shows what actually changed.

properties.name: Runs on Linux and macOS developer machines
properties.statement: Target platforms are Linux and macOS. Consumers must not pay a RocksDB C++ rebuild for a routine reflow2 update; the core runs on an in-memory backend with RocksDB opt-in.

properties.name: Released equals designed
properties.statement: At release the design must describe what was actually released.

properties.name: A design survives a reflow2 upgrade
properties.statement: An existing graph opens, or is refused loudly with what to do.

properties.name: Brownfield adopt trial on storyflow (2643 files): full RE lifecycle through the adopt skill, 5 true findings about storyflow, 4 about reflow2
skill_lint.py — the skills' contract with the served surface (tool refs resolve, mirrors byte-identical, frontmatter, standing rule), enforced in CI on every push
Schema validation and graph-store round-trip suite (cargo test --no-default-features: schema, nodes, graph, storage tests)
